nodes.comment: SUBSTITUTE FOR BETAMON (US-133). The V1 tree's second Rookie under Koromon is Betamon, which is one of the 157 idle-only Digimon: no 48x64 sheet, so it cannot be playable and cannot sit on an edge. Its two Champions that Agumon does not also reach - Airdramon and Seadramon - would then be unreachable, so this line carries them under Swimmon, a playable Water Rookie that neither Part of Digimon_Color_And_Pendulum_Color_Evolution_Trees.md names, so no later device tree loses a Rookie to it. Betamon's other three Champions (Devimon, Meramon, Numemon) are on Agumon's side of the tree already.

nodes.comment: ONE RUNG ABOVE WHERE THE DOCUMENT PUTS IT, and this is the story's one stage divergence. The V2 tree lists Whamon as a Champion feeding MetalMamemon, but this asset pack files its only animated sheet under Perfect/Whamon.png, so the roster has it at Perfect and a node claiming Adult would resolve to no art at all. Rather than drop a named Digimon, it stands BESIDE MetalMamemon instead of beneath it, off the same two Champions the document puts on its row (Garurumon and Yukidarumon), and shares MetalMamemon's Mega - the invented arrow, needed because a terminal Perfect is a Digimon that can never evolve again. Pendulum Color V2 Deep Savers puts Whamon at Perfect too (Rukamon -> Whamon -> Plesiomon), so US-139 will need a line-scoped id for it.

nodes.comment: DIVERGENCE FROM THE SOURCE TREE (US-044). The Digital Monster Color V3 tree (Resources/Digimon_Color_And_Pendulum_Color_Evolution_Trees.md) starts this line at Fresh: Poyomon, but Poyomon exists only as a single 16x16 frame in 'Idle Frame Only/' with no 48x64 animated sheet (verified in docs/sprite-availability.md), so it is dexOnly and may never be playable or named by an edge. Puttimon is substituted as the Baby I: it is the closest animated stand-in, being the Baby I of the same Angel family Patamon belongs to. Poyomon is the ONLY divergence in this line; every other node is the V3 tree as written.

The V3 tree gives Patamon five Champions and US-135 authors all five here, where US-044 could only fit three: four earned branches, one per energy type, plus Scumon. Scumon is the tree's junk Champion — the one BOTH Rookies fall to — so it is the isDefault edge, and a Patamon that qualifies for nothing else lands there. It shares Unimon's spirit gate but sits below it on minEnergy, so a well-raised spirit Patamon still reaches Unimon. Tsukaimon, which carried Ogremon and Bakemon between US-061 and US-135, keeps them: it is not in the source tree at all and would otherwise have been left with no branches of its own.

DIVERGENCES FROM THE SOURCE TREE (US-045, revised by US-136). (1) Two of Piyomon's five Champions are dropped for want of art: Kokatorimon is absent from the asset pack entirely, and Nanimon exists only as a single idle frame, so neither may be named by an edge (docs/sprite-availability.md). What is left of the document's row is Monochromon / Leomon / Kuwagamon, and that is exactly what this node offers. (2) Coelamon and Mojyamon are NOT Piyomon's in the source tree — they are the other Rookie's, Palmon's. US-045 hung them off the invented Hyokomon because the plain `palmon` id already belonged to the classic Palmon -> Togemon -> Lilimon line; US-136 added `dmcv4_palmon` and gave that Rookie its four drawable Champions, leaving Hyokomon its two rather than emptying it into a Child that evolves into nothing. (3) The tree's unearned Champion is Nanimon, which cannot be seeded, so the isDefault slot has no natural occupant: US-061's GoldNumemon takes it, sharing Leomon's strength gate from below on minEnergy and maxCareMistakes. A well-raised strength Piyomon still reaches Leomon.

The first Baby II in this file that BRANCHES. Five Champions and four energy types, the same squeeze as Patamon and Piyomon. Unlike Piyomon this line HAS a natural occupant for the isDefault slot: Raremon, a heap of rotting scrap, is the V5 tree's junk Champion in the way Scumon is V3's. It shares DarkTyranomon's strength gate from below on minEnergy and maxCareMistakes, so a well-raised strength Gazimon still reaches DarkTyranomon and only a neglected one lands on Raremon. US-137 finished the row the document draws — DarkTyranomon / Cyclomon / Devidramon / Tuskmon / Raremon — by adding Devidramon on spirit and Tuskmon on stamina. Every one of the five is drawable, so the row is four earned branches plus the junk fallback: one per energy type, which is the widest a Child can be, and no ceiling had to move.

The Pendulum Color V1 Nature Spirits tree's egg. Rooting the line at a Rookie's egg rather than at the Baby I's is also what US-045 did: the dmc-v4 line is rooted at Piyo Digitama and hatches to Yuramon.

Its earned edge back to MetalGreymon is the redemption path US-061 gave Numemon: expensive, but a neglected Digimon is never a dead end.

Like every junk Champion since US-138 it keeps one earned way back UP, so a player who neglected a Rookie and then worked is not locked out: the climb to Asuramon is this app's choice on flavour rather than a Wikimon arrow - Turuiemon is a Beast Man and Asuramon heads the Beast Man thread of this tree.

Candmon's egg. Candmon is already a Rookie of this line (`petimeramon` -> `candmon`), so its egg roots the line that reaches it.

Wikimon lists Betamon as evolving from Pukamon, and `pukamon` is this line's In-Training, so the Deep Savers line is the honest home.

Two eggs on one Baby I is deliberate - a Baby I may have several in-edges, and each egg still has exactly one hatch edge.
Impmon's egg. Wikimon: Kiimon evolves to Yarmon (Yaamon), and Impmon evolves from Yarmon, so Kiimon is Impmon's own Baby I. Impmon is a Tamers partner, hence this line.

Blucomon's egg. Wikimon: Cocomon evolves to Chocomon, and Blucomon evolves from Chocomon. Chocomon is also Lopmon's In-Training, which is why Blucomon rides the Tamers thread's baby rungs rather than opening a line of its own - US-145's lop_digitama hatches onto this same Cocomon.
Guilmon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Gigimon, which US-147 wires - see the dead-end ledger in DigitamaSweepTests. US-144 authors the Digitama rung only.
Impmon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Yaamon, which US-147 wires.
The Baby I under Chocomon, which is Lopmon's and Blucomon's In-Training. A LEAF for now: US-147 wires Chocomon.

Gaomon's egg, and the root of the beast-cub line. Wikimon: Dodomon evolves to Wanyamon, and Gaomon evolves from Wanyamon. Dodomon is also Dorumon's Baby I, which costs nothing - a Baby I may carry several out-edges, so US-148 can hang Dorumon's thread off the same node.
Bearmon's egg. Wikimon: Popomon evolves to Wanyamon, and Bearmon evolves from Wanyamon. Popomon rather than Dodomon so the two cubs do not share a Baby I while their Baby II already does.
Koemon's egg. Wikimon: Koemon evolves from Wanyamon, which is what Popomon leads to. Beside bear_digitama on the same Baby I.
Gaomon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Wanyamon, which US-147 wires.
Bearmon's and Koemon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Wanyamon, which US-147 wires.

Keramon's egg, and the root of the network-virus line that ends at Diablomon. Wikimon: Kuramon evolves to Tsumemon, and Keramon evolves from both Kuramon and Tsumemon, so Kuramon is Keramon's own Baby I.
Keramon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Tsumemon, which US-147 wires.

Commandramon's egg. Wikimon lists Commandramon as evolving from Bommon and from Missimon, so Bommon is its own Baby I. Missimon has no sheet in this app, so the Baby II above Bommon is US-147's call - Wikimon gives it Kyokyomon and Caprimon as alternatives.
Commandramon's Baby I. A LEAF for now, and the one whose canonical Baby II (Missimon) has NO sheet on disk - US-147 has to pick a stand-in rather than a straight lookup.
Ghostmon's egg. Wikimon lists Ghostmon as evolving from Algomon (Baby II), and Algomon (Baby I) is that Digimon's own rung below, so the Algomon spine is the thread that reaches Ghostmon.
Algomon's Baby I, the bottom of the Algomon spine. A LEAF for now: Algomon (Baby II) is US-147's.

Lalamon's egg, and the first of US-145's threads to join a line by its Baby II rather than by its Baby I. Wikimon: Pipimon evolves to Tanemon, and Lalamon evolves from Tanemon - and `tanemon` is already this line's Baby II, so `pipimon` is authored here as a SECOND Baby I of the plant line and US-147 closes the gap with an edge to a node that already exists. Lalamon is a plant Digimon, so the line is right on flavour as well as on the arrow.

Liollmon's egg. Liollmon has NO sheet in `16x16 Digimon Sprites` at all, so the species can never be on an edge and the egg is placed by where that species belongs: Wikimon draws Liollmon from Frimon, and Frimon from Popomon, which is this line's Baby I. Doubles up on `popomon` beside bear_digitama and koe_digitama rather than opening a rung nothing can climb.

Lopmon's egg. US-144 predicted this egg would double up on `cocomon`; it does not, and the reason is the allocation: 23 eggs were left for 25 orphaned Baby I, so an egg that CAN open one must. Wikimon: Relemon evolves to Moonmon, and Lopmon evolves from Moonmon - the arrow is drawn in both directions on both pages. Lopmon is Suzie's Tamers partner, which is the line.

Lucemon's egg. Wikimon: Tsubumon evolves to Tokomon, and Lucemon evolves from Tokomon - and `tokomon` is already this line's Baby II, so `tsubumon` joins as a second Baby I of Ver.3 and US-147 can close the dead end with an edge to a node that exists.

Ludomon's egg, and the root of the Vital Bracelet line. Wikimon: Cotsucomon evolves to Kakkinmon, and Ludomon evolves from Kakkinmon. Ludomon is drawn on Digimon Pendulum Ver.20th and Vital Bracelet Digital Monster, which is what this line collects.

Meicoomon's egg. Meicoomon's OWN Baby I, `meicoo_baby`, is one of the 157 idle-only Digimon, so this thread cannot be opened at the bottom however much the allocation would like it to be. Wikimon lists Keramon first among Meicoomon's Evolves From, and Keramon's Baby I here is `kuramon`, so the egg joins the network-virus line rather than inventing a rung.

Monodramon's egg. Wikimon: Ketomon evolves to Hopmon, and Monodramon evolves from Hopmon. Monodramon is Ryo's Tamers partner, which is the line. NOTE FOR US-147: Ketomon's only Evolves To is Hopmon and `hopmon` is idle-only, so this Baby I needs a stand-in Baby II rather than a straight lookup - the same problem `bommon` has.
Morphomon's egg. Wikimon: Bubbmon evolves to Mochimon, and Morphomon evolves from Mochimon. Morphomon is drawn on Pendulum Z Nature Spirits and Vital Bracelet BE.

Mushmon's egg. Mushmon is already a Child of this line, so by the sweep's first rule the egg roots the line that reaches it and adds no rung; Wikimon draws Mushmon from Nyokimon, which is this line's Baby I, so the arrow agrees with the placement.

PawnChessmon (Black)'s egg. Both PawnChessmon colours are idle-only, so neither species can be on an edge. Wikimon draws BOTH from Budmon, and Budmon from Botamon, which is this line's Baby I - so the pair is placed together on Ver.1 rather than split by colour.
PawnChessmon (White)'s egg, beside pawnchessblack_digitama by the variant rule and by the same Wikimon arrow: Budmon evolves to both colours, and Budmon evolves from Botamon.
Phascomon's egg. Wikimon: Choromon evolves to Caprimon, and Phascomon evolves from Caprimon - `caprimon` is this line's Baby II and `choromon` its Baby I, so the whole arrow is already drawn here.

PicoDevimon's egg. PicoDevimon is already a Child of this line, so the egg adds no rung; Wikimon draws PicoDevimon from Peti Meramon, and Peti Meramon from Mokumon, which are this line's Baby II and Baby I.
Plotmon's egg. Plotmon is already a Child of this line, so the egg adds no rung; Wikimon lists Yukimi Botamon among Plotmon's Evolves From directly, and `yukimibotamon` is this line's Baby I.
Pulsemon's egg. Wikimon: Dokimon evolves to Bibimon, and Pulsemon evolves from Bibimon. Pulsemon is drawn on Vital Bracelet Digital Monster and Vital Bracelet BE, which is this line.

Renamon's egg. Renamon's best-known Baby I is Relemon, which lop_digitama took; Wikimon also draws Renamon from Onibimon, and Onibimon from Tomorimon, so this thread is a real arrow and not a leftover. Renamon is Ruki's Tamers partner.

Sunarizamon's egg. Wikimon: Sunamon evolves to Goromon, and Goromon's only Evolves To is Sunarizamon - the tightest thread in this sweep. Sunarizamon is drawn on Pendulum Z Nature Spirits and Vital Bracelet Digital Monster.
Swimmon's egg. Swimmon is already a Child of this line, so the egg adds no rung; Wikimon draws Swimmon from Pinamon, and Pinamon from Botamon, which is this line's Baby I.
Terriermon's egg. Wikimon: Zerimon evolves to Gummymon, and Terriermon evolves from Gummymon. Terriermon is Jenrya's Tamers partner, beside Guilmon and Renamon on this line.
V-mon's egg, and the root of the Adventure 02 partners' line. Wikimon: Chicomon evolves to Chibimon, and V-mon evolves from Chibimon.

Vorvomon's egg. Wikimon draws Vorvomon from Peti Meramon, and Peti Meramon from Mokumon - this line's Baby II and Baby I - so the egg joins Nightmare Soldiers beside picodevi_digitama rather than opening a thread for a species that is already reachable through no rung of its own.
Wormmon's egg. Wikimon: Leafmon evolves to Minomon, and Wormmon evolves from Minomon. Wormmon is Ken's Adventure 02 partner, beside V-mon.

Zubamon's egg. Zubamon is idle-only and can never be on an edge, so the egg is placed by where the species belongs: Wikimon draws Zubamon from Kakkinmon, which is exactly what `cotsucomon` leads to, so it doubles up on the Baby I ludo_digitama opened rather than taking one a species with a sheet still needs.
Lalamon's Baby I. A LEAF for now, but the shortest one in the file: its Baby II is Tanemon, which is ALREADY on this line, so US-147 closes this dead end with an edge rather than a node.
Lucemon's Baby I. A LEAF for now, and the same shape as `pipimon`: its Baby II is Tokomon, which is already this line's, so US-147 needs only the edge.
Lopmon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Moonmon, which US-147 wires.
Monodramon's Baby I. A LEAF for now, and the one whose canonical Baby II (Hopmon) is idle-only - US-147 has to pick a stand-in rather than a straight lookup, the same problem `bommon` has.
Renamon's Baby I by way of Onibimon. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Onibimon, which US-147 wires.
Terriermon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Gummymon, which US-147 wires.
V-mon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Chibimon, which US-147 wires.
Wormmon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Minomon, which US-147 wires.
Pulsemon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Bibimon, which US-147 wires.
Morphomon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Mochimon, which US-147 wires.
Sunarizamon's Baby I. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Goromon, which US-147 wires.
Ludomon's and Zubamon's Baby I - two eggs on one Baby I, which is what the sweep does when the second species is idle-only. A LEAF for now: its Baby II is Kakkinmon, which US-147 wires.

Algomon's Baby II, the rung above US-144's algomon_babyi. Wikimon's Labramon page lists 'Algomon (Baby II)' among its Evolves From, which is also where Xiaomon goes - the two threads of this line converge on Labramon.
Lopmon's Baby II (Wikimon: Chocomon Evolves From Cocomon), the rung above US-144's cocomon.
The line's namesake, and the Baby II Wikimon puts above BOTH of US-144's dead ends here (Wanyamon Evolves From Botamon, Dodomon, Popomon). Curimon joins it too - see that node.
Guilmon's Baby II (Wikimon: Gigimon Evolves From Jyarimon), the rung above US-144's jyarimon.
Impmon's Baby II (Wikimon: Yaamon Evolves From Keemon, Mokumon), the rung above US-144's kiimon. Pafumon joins it too - see that node.
Keramon's Baby II (Wikimon: Tsumemon Evolves From Kuramon), the rung above US-144's kuramon.
Wikimon puts Mochimon above Bubbmon, which is US-145's bubbmon on this line. Pyonmon joins it too - see that node.
V-mon's Baby II (Wikimon: Chibimon Evolves From Chicomon), the rung above US-145's chicomon.
Wikimon: Kakkinmon Evolves From Cotsucomon, and Evolves To Ludomon and Zubamon - the two species whose eggs US-145 spent on cotsucomon.
Wikimon: Bibimon Evolves From Dokimon and Evolves To Pulsemon, which is the species US-145's pulse_digitama is named for.

Ketomon's stand-in Baby II. Ketomon's own is Hopmon, one of the 157 idle-only Digimon, so no edge may name it; Wikimon gives Dorimon as the ONLY Baby II on disk that evolves into Monodramon, which is what Hopmon evolves into and what monodra_digitama is named for.
Wormmon's Baby II (Wikimon: Minomon Evolves From Leafmon, Evolves To Wormmon), the rung above US-145's leafmon and the reason worm_digitama was paired with it.
Wikimon: Moonmon Evolves From Relemon - the pairing US-145's lop_digitama rests on.
Wikimon: Goromon Evolves From Sunamon and Evolves To Sunarizamon, which is the species US-145's sunariza_digitama is named for.
Renamon's Baby II by Wikimon's arrows: Onibimon Evolves From Tomorimon and Evolves To Renamon, which is the species US-145's rena_digitama is named for.
Terriermon's Baby II (Wikimon: Gummymon Evolves From Zerimon), the rung above US-145's zerimon.

Bombmon's Baby II by FLAVOUR, not by citation, and said plainly: Wikimon gives Bombmon no ordinary evolution at all - its Evolves To is three DigiXros fusions and nothing else - so there is no arrow to follow. Monimon is the pairing that costs least: both are Xros Wars Digimon that appear in the Digimon Xros Loader virtual pet, which is what this line is.
Wikimon: Pickmon Evolves From Chibickmon. Both appear in the Digimon Xros Loader virtual pet, which is what this line is.
Wikimon: Babydmon Evolves From Petitmon, and Evolves To Gumdramon - the Xros Wars Hunters partner - as well as Dracomon, itself a Digimon Xros Loader pet.
Wikimon: Puroromon Evolves From Pupumon, and Evolves To Ryudamon, which is also where Kyokyomon on this line goes.
Wikimon: Mococomon Evolves From Fukamon. Both appear in the Pendulum COLOR 6 Saiyu Warriors virtual pet, which is what this line is.
Wikimon: Xiaomon Evolves From Paomon and Evolves To Labramon, which is also where Algomon (Baby II) on this line goes.
Hawkmon's Baby II (Wikimon: Poromon Evolves From Pururumon, Evolves To Hawkmon), which is what puts it on the Adventure 02 line.
Wikimon: Pusurimon Evolves From Pusumon, and Evolves To Herissmon and nothing else - and herissmon is already a node on this line, so this is the only line it can sit on.
Wikimon: Kyokyomon Evolves From Fufumon, and Evolves To Commandramon - the species this line is named for and aiming at. That second arrow is also why Bommon evolves here.

One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach: US-144 and US-145 spent all 57 eggs and an egg carries exactly one hatch edge, so this rung is authored for its OUT-edge only. Wikimon gives Bombmon no ordinary evolution - see monimon for the flavour pairing. Bombmon is a Digimon Xros Wars Digimon and appears in the Digimon Xros Loader virtual pet, which is what this line is.
One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Wikimon: Chibickmon Evolves To Pickmon. Both appear in the Digimon Xros Loader virtual pet, which is what this line is.

One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Wikimon: Petitmon Evolves To Babydmon, and nothing else. Babydmon's own Evolves To reaches Gumdramon, the Digimon Xros Wars: Hunters partner, which is what puts this thread on the Xros Loader line.
One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Wikimon: Fufumon Evolves To Kyokyomon, and nothing else.
One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Wikimon: Pupumon Evolves To Puroromon, and nothing else.

One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Wikimon: Fukamon Evolves To Mococomon, and nothing else. Both appear in the Pendulum COLOR 6 Saiyu Warriors virtual pet, which is what this line is.
One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Wikimon: Paomon Evolves To Xiaomon and Labramon; the Baby II is the rung that advances one stage.

One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Wikimon: Pururumon Evolves To Nyaromon and Poromon; Nyaromon is already fed by yukimibotamon on penc-vb, so Poromon is the arrow that opens something.

One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Its own Baby II, Gurimon, is not in the sprite pack at all, so this is a REHOME: Wikimon has Gurimon evolve into Koemon, and Wanyamon is the Baby II on disk that also evolves into Koemon.

One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Its own Baby II, Kyaromon, is one of the 157 idle-only Digimon, so no edge may name it; this is a REHOME on Wikimon's Kyaromon -> Impmon, which is also where Yaamon goes.

One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Its own Baby II, Bosamon, is not in the sprite pack, so this is a REHOME on Wikimon's Bosamon -> Angoramon, which is also where Mochimon goes. Pyonmon is a Vital Bracelet Digital Monster, which is this line.
One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Wikimon: Pusumon Evolves To Pusurimon, and nothing else; Pusurimon's only Child is Herissmon, already a node here.

One of the thirteen Baby I no Digitama can reach - see bombmon. Its own Baby II, Puyoyomon, is not in the sprite pack, so this is a REHOME on Wikimon's Puyoyomon -> Jellymon: Pukamon is the only Baby II on disk that also evolves into Jellymon, and this file already draws that arrow.
